Zhibo Xuan (宣智博)

Postdoc & Lecturer · School of Computer Science and Engineering, Beihang University

zhibo.xuan [at] buaa.edu.cn

Email/ Google Scholar/ GitHub/ ORCID

portrait

I am a postdoc and lecturer at the School of Computer Science and Engineering, Beihang University, where I received my Ph.D. in Computer Science under the supervision of Prof. Hailong Yang. I also received my B.S. in Computer Science from Beihang University.

My research lies at the intersection of computer architecture and machine-learning systems. I work on high-performance computing, performance analysis and tuning tools, deep-learning systems, and training & inference systems for large language models — in particular, how the next generation of AI workloads should reshape the hardware and software stacks beneath them.

Some of my recent work includes profiling tools (GVARP, TrivialSpy) for diagnosing performance bottlenecks, variance, and inefficiencies, and systems for elastic training and sparse-embedding acceleration (EasyScale, RecServe).

News

  • Apr 2026Our paper on [LLM kernel scheduling] was accepted to [Conference '26].
  • Feb 2026Gave a talk at [Workshop / Seminar Name] on profiling memory bandwidth for transformer inference.
  • Dec 2025Started a research internship at [Industry Lab], working on [topic].
  • Sep 2025Joined the [Lab Name] as a research assistant under Prof. [Advisor].
  • Jun 2025Received the [Outstanding Undergraduate Thesis] award from [University].

Research Interests

High-performance computing

Systems and runtime optimization for large-scale scientific workloads.

Performance analysis & tuning tools

Profiling methodologies for diagnosing performance bottlenecks, variance, and inefficiencies (e.g., GVARP, TrivialSpy).

Deep learning systems

Training/inference optimization, elastic training, sparse-embedding acceleration (e.g., EasyScale, RecServe).

Training & inference systems for LLMs

Efficient LLM serving and training on heterogeneous hardware.

Education

Ph.D.Computer Science, Beihang University — advisor: Prof. Hailong Yang
B.S.Computer Science, Beihang University

Experience

Postdoc, Lecturer, School of Computer Science and Engineering, Beihang University

Publications

Profiling and Optimizing Mixed-Precision Attention Kernels on Modern GPUs

Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor A], [Coauthor B], Hailong Yang

Under review, 2026PDFDB

· Identifies three latency tail sources in FlashAttention-style kernels and proposes a scheduling fix that recovers ~14% end-to-end throughput.

A Roofline-Style Model for LLM Decoding on Heterogeneous Accelerators

Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor C], Hailong Yang

SC '25, 2025CCF-APDFDB

· A first-principles performance model that predicts decoding throughput within 8% across four hardware platforms.

Cache-Aware Sparse Attention Scheduling for Long-Context Inference

[Coauthor D], Zhibo Xuan, Hailong Yang

ICS '25, 2025CCF-BPDFDB

· A reordering of sparse-attention compute that reduces L2 misses by 2.1× on A100, with no accuracy change.

An Empirical Study of Memory Bandwidth Bottlenecks in Transformer Training

Zhibo Xuan, Hailong Yang

TPDS, 2024CCF-APDFDB

· An empirical analysis of bandwidth saturation across attention, MLP and norm layers; informs the design of GVARP.

Communication-Efficient Tensor Parallelism for Trillion-Scale Models

[Coauthor E], Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor F], Hailong Yang

HPCA '24, 2024CCF-APDFDB

· Hides 73% of cross-node tensor-parallel traffic behind compute via a layered overlap schedule.

GVARP: Profiling Performance Variance for Large-Scale GPU Workloads

Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor G], Hailong Yang

SC '23, 2023CCF-APDFDB

· Lightweight profiler that pinpoints the sources of run-to-run performance variance in HPC GPU jobs.

TrivialSpy: Identifying Trivial Computations for Performance Tuning

Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor H], Hailong Yang

ICS '23, 2023CCF-BPDFDB

· Detects redundant / trivial computations at instruction granularity to guide programmer-driven tuning.

EasyScale: Elastic Training with Consistent Convergence

[Coauthor I], Zhibo Xuan, Hailong Yang

SC '22, 2022CCF-APDFDB

· Makes elastic training reproducible — same convergence trajectory regardless of resource scaling events.

RecServe: Sparse-Embedding Acceleration for Recommendation Inference

Zhibo Xuan, [Coauthor J], Hailong Yang

IPDPS '22, 2022CCF-BPDFDB

· GPU-side sparse-embedding cache that lifts recommendation inference throughput by 3.4× on production traces.
